title: Basic Use of Dewalt DW716 Miter Saw in Biological and Agricultural Engineering Labs (BAEL)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c4qsp","text":"This protocol provides basic operating procedures for safe and effective use of a Dewalt DW716 Miter Saw in a student machine shop setting. It outlines required materials, safety precautions, and step-by-step instructions for making accurate crosscuts and angled cuts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6pojp","text":"Only trained users may operate the miter saw","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"28h8s","text":"Always wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nj99","text":"Maintain a clean and organized workspace","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b8roi","text":"Inspect all materials and equipment before use","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1evd","text":"Use the correct blade for the material being cut","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2dcd3","text":"Verify the saw is unplugged before inspection","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"es6ds","text":"Inspect blade for damage, dullness, or debris","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3tg6k","text":"Ensure blade guard moves freely and returns automatically","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bncin","text":"Confirm fence and table are clean and aligned","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"epnju","text":"Check that material is free of nails, screws, or defects","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"el202","text":"Adjust miter and bevel angles as needed before powering on","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cafh2","text":"Always adjust fence properly before use","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4278b","text":"Clamp small pieces before cutting","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"eu4n4","text":"DeWALT DW716 Miter Saw, 12 in Dia Blade, 2 x 8 in 45 deg, 2 x 10 in at 90 deg Cutting Capacity, 3800 rpm Speed","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f6ugj","text":"Appropriate saw blade","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f08u8","text":"Workpiece material (wood, plastic, etc.)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b9i28","text":"Clamps (if needed for stability)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a2pk0","text":"Measuring tape or ruler","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ce78","text":"Pencil for marking cuts","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"535vv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9dtrq","text":"Personal Protective Equipment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fia7l","text":"Safety glasses","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7prkr","text":"Closed-toe shoes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ffnnj","text":"Hearing protection (recommended)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: PPE Check Ensure that you have all Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Safety glasses Closed-toe shoes Hearing protection (recommended)

Setup equipment Check electrical cable and be sure it is free from damage (e.g., exposed wire) If necessary, plug in the saw to a 120V 60Hz outlet. Ensure stable placement of the miter saw on the workbench (equipment should not be wobbling with a gentle shake)

Position saw (optional): If cutting material at an angle, set desired miter angle (left/right rotation) ( Fig 1A ) (optional): If cutting material at an angle, set bevel angle if required (tilt of blade) ( Fig 1B ) Using a pencil or marker, mark cut line(s) clearly on the material. Figure 1 . Position saw for: A) Left/right rotation by adjusting miter angle and B) Tilt of blade by adjusting bevel angle.

Position material Place material flat on the table Use one hand to securely hold the workpiece against the fence ( Fig 2A ). Use the other hand to operate the saw handle Align blade with cut mark (without powering on saw) (optional): Use clamps to firmly position material Critical note: Do not cut extremely small pieces that cannot be safely secured. It is highly recommended to use clamps for securing workpiece Figure 2 . Position material against the fence

Perform cut Raise the blade clear of the material before starting the saw Press and hold the trigger to start the blade Allow blade to reach full speed. Critical note: Listen for a constant hum from the motor. If the motor sounds abnormal, slows unexpectedly, vibrates excessively, or struggles during cutting, stop immediately and notify shop personnel. Double check that no loose clothing may interact with the saw Keep hands away from the sawblade at all times Using right hand, slowly lower the blade through the material Maintain steady pressure-do not force the cut Complete the cut thorugh the material Release the trigger to stop the blade Allow the blade to stop before raising it.
Finish Cut Keep blade down until it stops spinning Raise blade slowly after it has stopped Remove material carefully Inspect cut for accuracy

Clean Up Turn off power to the saw Remove debris from table and surrounding area Inspect cord for damage and store neatly away from walkways Store equipment properly Return reusable PPE (e.g., safety glasses, hearing protection) to proper location
creator: Eric S S McLamore
